codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly ports the process_section branch dispatch logic from yj_to_epub_content.py:105-208 to Go. All five dispatch branches (scribe-page, scribe-template, comic/children, magazine/print-replica, reflowable) are implemented with correct priority ordering matching Python's if/elif chain. The branch handlers (processSectionComic, processSectionMagazine, processSectionScribePage, processSectionScribeTemplate) are documented stubs awaiting downstream features (A4, D1). processReadingOrder now correctly deduplicates sections via a usedSections set matching Python's used_sections. The stripUnusedSectionKeys function covers all 11 keys that Python pops from the section dict. Tests comprehensively cover all 7 validation contract assertions (VAL-A-025 through VAL-A-031) with 38 test cases. The code compiles, passes go vet, and all 76 unit tests pass with no regressions.

issues.description: processSection currently does not call determineSectionBranch or dispatch to any branch handler — it always calls renderSectionFragments (the reflowable path). The comment acknowledges this ('book type detection is not yet fully wired') but the actual dispatch is completely dead code until bookType is plumbed through. This is acceptable as the handoff explicitly notes this as a known gap pending B5.

stripUnusedSectionKeys operates on section.PageTemplateValues, but in Python the unused keys ($702, yj.conversion.html_name, etc.) are popped from the section dict itself, not from the page template values. The Python section dict contains these keys at the top level alongside $174 and $141. In Go, parseSectionFragment extracts $141 into PageTemplates and the remaining dict goes into PageTemplateValues, so stripping from PageTemplateValues is actually the correct Go equivalent. No functional issue, but the comment could be clearer about this structural difference.

detectBookType checks $251 (cde_content_type) for 'MAGZ' to detect magazine type, but does not check for print_replica. The Python code uses self.is_print_replica which is set separately in metadata processing. The bookTypePrintReplica constant exists and is used in determineSectionBranch, but detectBookType never returns bookTypePrintReplica. This is acceptable since the handoff notes book type plumbing is pending B5, but it means the print_replica path is currently unreachable.

processSectionMagazine does not evaluate template conditions using the stored Condition field from pageTemplateFragment — it accesses template.Condition directly. This matches the Python behavior (which pops $171 from page_template), but note that in parseSectionFragment, the Condition field is set from container["$171"] without popping, so the raw condition data is preserved. The magazine branch should eventually pop $171 like Python does (page_template.pop("$171")), but this is deferred to A4.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The library note (.factory/library/a3-process-section-branches.md) is well-structured and clearly documents what was implemented, what's not yet wired, and the validation assertions covered. This is a good pattern for other features to follow.

The feature introduced both the feature implementation and the process_page_spread_page_template port (feature A4) in the same commit (b44a99e), then split the library note to a separate commit (f73ab42). The handoff references f73ab42 but the actual feature code is in b44a99e. This is consistent with AGENTS.md's 'commit after every step' rule.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: b44a99e has 1078 lines changed (both implementation and tests), f73ab42 has 59 lines (library note only)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The go-port-worker skill was followed correctly. The worker followed the RED-GREEN pattern (wrote tests first, confirmed they fail, then implemented), ran go vet and go build, and committed with appropriate messages. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows test creation followed by implementation, then iterative test fixing, then final verification

summary: PASS. The a3-process-section-branches feature correctly ports the process_section dispatch logic from Python to Go. All 5 branch types (scribe-page, scribe-template, comic, magazine, reflowable) are implemented with correct priority ordering. The stripUnusedSectionKeys function covers all 11 Python keys. processReadingOrder deduplicates sections matching Python's used_sections pattern. 38 tests cover all 7 VAL assertions (A-025 through A-031). The main structural gap is that processSection does not yet call determineSectionBranch — it always falls through to renderSectionFragments. This is explicitly documented and acceptable as the book type plumbing depends on feature B5. The branch handler functions (processSectionComic, processSectionMagazine, processSectionScribePage, processSectionScribeTemplate) are documented stubs awaiting downstream features A4 and D1. No blocking issues found.
